title: Critical open defects entering UAT without a minimum closure threshold
detail: BUG-902 (duplicate charge, CRITICAL) and two HIGH-severity bugs are open at UAT entry. No defect-closure gate is documented, so release can proceed regardless of outstanding issues.

title: No security acceptance criteria — PCI-DSS and OWASP sign-off absent before go-live
detail: The UAT checklist contains no security sign-off requirements, no penetration-test evidence, and no PCI-DSS scope validation. Security owner is unnamed; legal/compliance is excluded from the distribution list.

title: Rollback procedure and post-release monitoring plan both marked TBD at hard go-live deadline
detail: The UAT sign-off sheet lists rollback procedure and monitoring plan as TBD with no contingency buffer. A hard marketing deadline with no documented fallback creates high deployment and operational risk.

title: Incomplete UAT scope — 9 functional areas deferred without risk acceptance
detail: Items 1.6–1.14 (currency conversion, chargebacks, partial refunds, subscriptions, promo-codes, invoicing) are deferred to a follow-up checklist that has not yet been written, with no risk sign-off from the product owner.

title: Deployment runbook missing rollback steps and environment-specific configuration
detail: The deployment runbook documents the happy path only. Rollback steps, environment variable differences between staging and production, and database migration reversal procedures are absent.

title: CI pipeline specification omits security scanning and integration test stages
detail: The pipeline spec defines build and unit-test stages but is missing SAST, DAST, dependency scanning, and integration test stages. Vulnerabilities introduced at code time are not caught before release.

title: Incident response plan in draft — escalation paths and on-call ownership undefined
detail: The incident response plan is marked DRAFT with no approval date. On-call rotation, severity-based escalation matrix, and external communication templates are all missing, leaving the team without a tested playbook.

title: Security review checklist incomplete — threat model and pentest scope absent
detail: The security review checklist covers only 40% of OWASP controls and contains no threat model, no pentest scope definition, and no sign-off criteria. High-risk attack surfaces (payment flows, auth) are unchecked.

title: No performance baselines or load-test targets defined for non-broadband scenarios
detail: Only a single broadband page-load threshold (< 2 s) is specified. Mobile (3G/4G), peak-load targets, and p99 latency thresholds are absent, making it impossible to verify acceptable performance before release.

title: Excluded UAT areas (fraud engine, webhook retries) lack documented risk justification
detail: Four high-value areas are explicitly excluded from UAT scope with no risk justification and no product owner sign-off for the exclusion. Defects in these areas may reach production undetected.
